task_completion.description: The configuration file is updated with the required identity validation settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal or text editor showing '/home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/custom-config.js' being modified to include 'prejoinPageEnabled: true' and 'requireDisplayName: true'.

task_completion.description: The Jitsi deployment is restarted to apply the configuration changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing commands executed in the '~/jitsi' directory to restart the services (e.g., 'docker-compose restart' or 'docker compose down' followed by 'up').

task_completion.description: The agent navigates to the target Jitsi meeting room in a web browser.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open with the URL bar showing 'http://localhost:8080/PolicyTestRoom'.

task_completion.description: The pre-join screen enforces identity validation by blocking entry without a name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser shows the Jitsi pre-join screen with an empty display name input field, and the 'Join meeting' button is visibly disabled or unclickable.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the blocked join state is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot tool or terminal command is used to save the blocked state image specifically to '~/Documents/evidence_blocked.png'.

task_completion.description: The agent successfully joins the meeting room using the required display name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser shows the active Jitsi meeting interface for 'PolicyTestRoom' with the participant's display name clearly shown as 'Verified User'.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the successful join state is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot tool or terminal command is used to save the successful join image specifically to '~/Documents/evidence_success.png'.

integrity.description: The agent uses a real web browser to interact with the Jitsi Meet instance.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a standard web browser UI rendering the Jitsi web application, not a mock-up or text-based browser.

integrity.description: The evidence screenshots are genuinely captured from the agent's session.
integrity.visual_evidence: The process of taking and saving the screenshots is visible in the trajectory, rather than the files magically appearing or being copied from an external source.

integrity.description: The agent does not use browser developer tools to fake the UI state.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's developer tools (Inspect Element) are not used to manually disable the join button or alter the display name in the DOM to fake the evidence.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task configures Jitsi Meet running at http://localhost:8080 to enforce identity validation. The configuration file is /home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/custom-config.js, where 'prejoinPageEnabled' and 'requireDisplayName' must be set to true. The target room is 'PolicyTestRoom'. The agent must save two screenshots to /home/ga/Documents/: 'evidence_blocked.png' showing the pre-join screen blocking entry when the name is empty, and 'evidence_success.png' showing successful entry with the name 'Verified User'.
